invariants.summary: The project/lab identity is Monterey Bay AI Lab. Do not call the project MBAL, MBAL AI, MBAL AI Lab, or MBAL AI Forecasting; MBAL is allowed only for factual source-data/provider references, URLs/domains, legacy compatibility file/module names, or existing compatibility env vars.
Agents may research and plan autonomously, but file modifications and state-changing commands require explicit user approval.
Heavy tasks, especially GPU jobs, must pass through ops/traffic_controller.py or ops/run_safe.py.
Agents must claim files before editing and release claims afterward.
Build like a lakehouse, but do not add Databricks product files, CLI dependencies, or DATABRICKS_* requirements.
Lakehouse metric dedup keys must include model and split_id.
Aggregate skill must use the median; means are reference-only because outliers can dominate.
Smoke runs must not be pooled into production leaderboards.
Promotion requires beating the better of persistence and seasonal-naive where seasonal evidence is available.
Do not auto-rewrite model code when loss spikes; halt or alert so a human can inspect possible physical anomalies.
Training target scaling must match evaluator un-scaling; avoid global-average training scaling when evaluation uses local context-window un-scaling.
Clip normalized targets, for example to [-20, 20], to prevent near-zero-variance sensor flatlines from exploding.
Safety monitors should use torch.cuda.memory_allocated(), not memory_reserved(), because the PyTorch caching allocator reserves memory aggressively.
